metadata.title: Points to Consider on Wording of Helicobacter Pylori Eradication Therapy in Selected SPC Sections

rules.rule_text: All claims for H. pylori eradication products must be scientifically justified with appropriate state-of-the-art clinical trials and argumentation.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: It goes without saying that all claims should be scientifically justified with the appropriate state of the art clinical trials and argumentation.
cross_references: CPMP guideline for fixed combination products

rules.rule_text: The rationale for the presence and contribution of each component to the efficacy of a new combination regimen for H. pylori eradication must be substantiated with appropriate data, including factorial design clinical studies where appropriate.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: The rationale for the presence and contribution of each component to the efficacy of a new combination regimen for H. pylori eradication should be substantiated with appropriate data. Relevant advice may be found in the CPMP guideline for fixed combination products. This implies that the clinical development programme, where appropriate, will have to include clinical studies with factorial design. In case this is not performed e.g. depending on the extent of knowledge of efficacy of some components of the regimen, this should be justified.
exceptions: Factorial design may be omitted if the extent of knowledge of efficacy of some components of the regimen justifies this; omission must be justified.
cross_references: CPMP guideline for fixed combination products

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory trials must be double-blind controlled trials with the positive control being the best available approved eradication regimen achieving eradication rate >80% (approaching 90%), and non-inferiority of the new regimen must be demonstrated.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: The confirmatory trials should be double-blind controlled trials. The positive control should be the best available approved eradication regimen, with eradication rate > 80% (approaching 90%) and non-inferiority of the new regimen should be demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: The lower limit of the 95% CI for the difference between eradication rates must be greater than the non-inferiority margin, which must be clinically justified and pre-specified in the protocol.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: The lower limit of the 95% CI for the difference between eradication rates should be greater than the non-inferiority margin which should be clinically justified and specified in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Consistency of effect must be demonstrated across several trials, and adherence to CPMP interaction and ICH statistical guidelines is mandatory.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: It is considered necessary to demonstrate consistency of effect in several trials. Adherence to the CPMP interaction and ICH statistical guidelines must be observed.

rules.rule_text: When approval of a new eradication regimen is sought alongside earlier approved regimens, a critical assessment of earlier regimens in light of new data must be provided; questioned regimens must be discouraged and their benefit-risk reviewed when safer or more efficacious alternatives exist.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: When addition of a new eradication regimen is sought besides earlier approved eradication regimen(s) critical assessment of the latter regimen(s) in the light of new data and state of the art should be given. Questioned regimens should be discouraged. This implies that the benefit-risk ratio of some earlier approved but now questioned regimens need to be reviewed when safer or more efficacious regimens become available.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials must account for the epidemiology of resistance of H. pylori to antibacterials, document rates of primary and secondary resistance, and reflect appropriateness of claimed regimens for use at different EU marketing sites; local clinical testing must be performed whenever indicated.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical trials should take into account the epidemiology of resistance of H. pylori to antibacterials. The rate of primary resistance and development of secondary resistance and clinical implications should be adequately documented and analysed. In other words, the trials should reflect the appropriateness of claimed eradication therapy regimens for use at different sites in the EU where the product is to be marketed. Local clinical testing will be performed whenever indicated.

rules.rule_text: Clinical studies must aim to find the shortest duration of antibiotic treatment that achieves an adequate eradication rate, in order to minimise undesirable effects associated with antibiotics and achieve an optimal benefit-risk ratio.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: The clinical studies should also aim at finding the shortest duration of antibiotic treatment to achieve required (adequate) eradication rate in order to decrease the risk of undesirable effects associated with the use of antibiotics, hence, resulting in optimal benefit-risk ratio.

rules.rule_text: For fixed galenical formulations of combination products developed for H. pylori eradication therapy, the CPMP guideline for fixed combination products applies, and the pharmacodynamic section must focus on susceptibility of H. pylori to the involved agents.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: For fixed galenical formulations of combination products developed for the H. pylori eradication therapy, the CPMP guideline for fixed combination products will be applicable. The requirements for specific information on individual components of the combination and the claimed dose regimen should be addressed appropriately, depending on the extent of knowledge of efficacy and safety of some components of the regimen... The information on antibacterial activity (in the Pharmacodynamic section) should focus on susceptibility of H. pylori to the involved agents.
cross_references: CPMP guideline for fixed combination products

rules.rule_text: The appropriate dose to be used in an indication for combination therapy must be mentioned in the individual product information of each component of the combination therapy.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: In principle, the appropriate dose to be used in an indication for combination therapy should be mentioned in the individual product information of each component of the combination therapy.

rules.rule_text: For H. pylori-associated conditions other than ulcer disease (e.g. gastritis with serious abnormalities, post early gastric cancer resection, MALT lymphoma), adequate evidence of efficacy and safety in those specific conditions is required for each proposed combination regimen.
rules.source_section: General Remarks
rules.verbatim_quote: For H. pylori associated conditions such as gastritis with serious abnormalities, post early gastric cancer resection, mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma (= MALToma) adequate evidence of efficacy and safety in such clinical conditions and for each proposed combination regimen must be evaluated in appropriate state of the art and regulatory settings.
exceptions: These conditions are not within the scope of this Points to Consider document, but similar SPC formatting as for management of H. pylori associated ulcer disease may be used.
cross_references: The European H. pylori Study Group, Maastricht Consensus (GUT 1997; 41: 8-13)

rules.rule_text: For Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), the SPC Section 4.1 (Therapeutic indications) must include the exact wording: 'In combination with appropriate antibacterial therapeutic regimens for the eradication of H. pylori and prevention of relapse of peptic ulcers in patients with H. pylori associated ulcers. See section 4.2'.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), Therapeutic indications (section 4.1)
rules.verbatim_quote: "In combination with appropriate antibacterial therapeutic regimens for the eradication of H. pylori and prevention of relapse of peptic ulcers in patients with H. pylori associated ulcers. See section 4.2"

rules.rule_text: Clinical documentation supporting the UHA indication must contain sufficiently representative clinical trials controlled with the best available approved eradication regimen, exploring the merits of the different combination regimens.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), Therapeutic indications (section 4.1)
rules.verbatim_quote: The documentation should contain sufficiently representative clinical trials controlled with the best available approved eradication regimen exploring the merits of the different combination regimens.

rules.rule_text: For Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), the SPC Section 4.2 (Posology and method of administration) must include the exact wording specifying treatment of H. pylori-infected patients with eradication therapy, basis for regimen selection, agent dose and frequency, duration, and a cross-reference to individual product data sheets for other eradication therapy components.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), Posology and method of administration (section 4.2)
rules.verbatim_quote: "Patients with gastro-duodenal ulcers due to H. pylori infection should be treated with eradication therapy with appropriate combinations of antibiotics with adequate dosing regimens. Selection of the appropriate regimen should be based on patient tolerability and therapeutic guidelines/ availability of the antibiotics. (Agent X) can be used in a dose of Y mg (twice or whatever appropriate) daily during the eradication therapy of ... week(s) duration, e.g.: Clarithromycin 200 mg BID + metronidazole 400 mg BID + UHA ... mg BID for 1 week. For further information on the other components of the eradication therapy see the individual product data sheet".

rules.rule_text: For Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), the SPC Section 4.5 (Interaction with other medicaments and other forms of interaction) must address the absence of or presence of interaction with relevant antibiotics used in eradication therapy, on the basis of gathered literature and prospectively studied combinations.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), Interaction with other medicaments and other forms of interaction (section 4.5)
rules.verbatim_quote: Absence of or presence of interaction with relevant antibiotics which are used in eradication therapy should be mentioned here on the basis of gathered literature and prospectively studied combinations.

rules.rule_text: For Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), the SPC Section 5.1 (Pharmacodynamic properties) must briefly mention the trial setting for the tested H. pylori eradication regimens, and must highlight results in subgroups with primary resistance and development of secondary resistance.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), Pharmacodynamic properties (section 5.1)
rules.verbatim_quote: Information on the trial setting used for testing the sought and approved H. pylori eradication regimens should be briefly mentioned. The results in subgroups with primary resistance to any of the involved antibacterial agents and development of secondary resistance should be highlighted.

rules.rule_text: For Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), Section 5.1 must include wording noting that local information on prevalence of resistance and local therapeutic guidelines should be taken into account in the choice of an appropriate combination regimen, and that secondary resistance development must be considered for retreatment decisions.
rules.source_section: Section 1 — Ulcer Healing Agents (UHA), Pharmacodynamic properties (section 5.1)
rules.verbatim_quote: Hence, local information on the prevalence of resistance and local therapeutic guidelines should be taken into account in the choice of an appropriate combination regimen for H. pylori eradication therapy. Furthermore, in patients with persistent infection, potential development of secondary resistance (in patients with primary susceptible strains) to an antibacterial agent should be taken into account in the considerations for a new retreatment regimen.

rules.rule_text: For Antibacterial Medicinal Products, the SPC Section 4.1 (Therapeutic indications) must follow the UHA category wording with the following adjustment: 'In appropriate combination ... (and an appropriate ulcer healing agent)...'.
rules.source_section: Section 2 — Antibacterial medicinal products, Therapeutic indications (section 4.1)
rules.verbatim_quote: As for category (1) with the following adjustment: "In appropriate combination ... (and an appropriate ulcer healing agent)...".

rules.rule_text: For Antibacterial Medicinal Products, SPC Sections 4.2 and 4.5 must follow the same requirements as for the UHA category.
rules.source_section: Section 2 — Antibacterial medicinal products, Posology and method of administration (section 4.2); Interaction with other medicaments (section 4.5)
rules.verbatim_quote: Posology and method of administration (section 4.2): As for category (1). Interaction with other medicaments and other forms of interaction (section 4.5): As for category (1).

rules.rule_text: For Antibacterial Medicinal Products, Section 5.1 must follow the UHA requirements and additionally include data on the prevalence of resistance of H. pylori to the antibacterials mentioned in the claimed eradication therapy regimens, as outlined in the CPMP Note for Guidance on the Pharmacodynamic Section of the SPC for Antibacterial Medicinal Products.
rules.source_section: Section 2 — Antibacterial medicinal products, Pharmacodynamic properties (section 5.1)
rules.verbatim_quote: As for category (1). In addition, data on the prevalence of resistance of H. pylori to the antibacterials mentioned in the claimed eradication therapy regimens should be listed as outlined in the CPMP Note for Guidance on the Pharmacodynamic Section of the SPC for Antibacterial Medicinal Products.
cross_references: CPMP Note for Guidance on the Pharmacodynamic Section of the SPC for Antibacterial Medicinal Products

rules.rule_text: For combination products (combi-packs) with the sole claimed indication of H. pylori eradication therapy, other antibacterial activities must not be mentioned in the SPC; instead, reference must be made to approved individual SPCs.
rules.source_section: Section 2 — Antibacterial medicinal products, Pharmacodynamic properties (section 5.1), Note
rules.verbatim_quote: Note: For combination products presentation of the information should be focused on the claimed sole indication "H. pylori eradication therapy", other antibacterial activities should not be mentioned here but reference should be made to approved individual SPCs.

rules.rule_text: For combi-pack presentations, data on resistance prevalence in Section 5.1 may be summarised using the following example wording: 'The prevalence of metronidazole-resistant H. pylori strains has been described to be 30-50% in Western Europe. The prevalence of resistant strains to amoxicillin and clarithromycin is significantly below that, with rates ...%.'
rules.source_section: Section 2 — Antibacterial medicinal products, Pharmacodynamic properties (section 5.1), Note
rules.verbatim_quote: For combi-pack presentations the data on resistance prevalence can be summarised e.g. as follows: "The prevalence of metronidazole-resistant H. pylori strains has been described to be 30-50% in Western Europe. The prevalence of resistant strains to amoxicillin and clarithromycin is significantly below that, with rates ...%."
exceptions: This is an example template; actual resistance rates must be filled in with current epidemiological data.
